_note: Hand-authored external figures used by the essay. kind: verified | illustrative. Every entry carries its source.

funding_cut_pct.label: real-terms cut in council core funding per resident since 2010-11

regressive.label: funding fell over twice as far in the poorest tenth of areas as in the richest — austerity by design

efs.label: emergency central support to councils in 2024/25, up from £79.9m in 2018/19 — a system on life support

care_leakage.label: a year leaks out of the UK care-home sector as profit, debt interest, rent and fees (~10% of income) — the market Devon's £3.5bn buys into

temp_accommodation.label: England's temporary-accommodation bill in 2024/25 (+118% in five years); councils' own unsubsidised share rose from 7% to 51%

exeter_city_living.label: delivered by Exeter's for-sale development company before it was wound up — £4.5m+ losses, ~£10.1m owed, against a 675-home target. The cautionary local lesson: build to rent (HRA), not to sell.

preston.label: extra anchor-institution spend redirected into Preston's local economy (5% → 18.2%)

cmi.label: raised directly from residents via local climate bonds across 8 councils since 2020 — cheaper than PWLB, but modest

land_value.label: of land-value uplift on new development goes uncaptured; >£8bn in developer contributions sits unspent

cautionary.label: debt at Woking and losses at Thurrock from speculative commercial bets — why borrowing to buy assets 'primarily for yield' was banned in 2020. Productive assets yes; financial speculation no.

rent_vs_build.source: Illustrative scenario — not a forecast; shows the structural logic of asset vs. liability
rent_vs_build.note: A one-off capital home (built/acquired) becomes a rent-yielding asset; nightly-paid temporary accommodation is a permanent, unsubsidised cost that recurs every year.
